title: mildly smoky turkish eggplant jjim
description: a smoky, crisp, and flavorful turkish imam bayildi dish featuring dark chocolate chips. serves well as a solo dinner.

ingredients.name: large eggplants
ingredients.notes: any variety, but prefering darker skin for better smokiness

ingredients.name: olive oil
ingredients.notes: use a good quality, extra virgin olive oil

ingredients.name: dark chocolate chips
ingredients.notes: at least 70% cocoa, for added depth

instructions: preheat the oven to 375 degrees fahrenheit.
cut the eggplants in half lengthwise and place them on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper.
drizzle the eggplants with olive oil, sprinkle with salt and black pepper, and gently rub them with your hands to coat evenly.
roast the eggplants in the oven for about 30 minutes, or until they're tender and lightly caramelized.
while the eggplants are roasting, heat the olive oil in a large skillet over medium heat.
add the minced garlic and cook, stirring occasionally, until it's lightly golden and fragrant.
add the sliced onion to the skillet and cook, stirring occasionally, until it's translucent and slightly caramelized.
remove the eggplants from the oven and let them cool slightly.
once the eggplants have cooled, scoop out the flesh and add it to the skillet with the onion and garlic.
stir in the smoked paprika, dark brown sugar, and dark chocolate chips.
season the mixture with salt and black pepper to taste, then stir to combine.
return the skillet to the stovetop and simmer the mixture over low heat for about 10 minutes, stirring occasionally.
